events.title: World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’
events.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine have reportedly killed and injured civilians and damaged critical infrastructure in Dnipro, Odesa, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia and other regions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’

events.title: LaGuardia pilots raised safety alarms months before deadly runway crash
events.description: Nasa reports show repeated warnings of close calls before crash that killed two pilots and injured 41 others Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Pilot safety concerns about New York ’s LaGuardia airport were filed to aviation officials months before Sunday’s collision between an airplane and a fire truck left two pilots dead and 41 other people hospitalized. According to the aviation safety reporting system administered by the US space agency Nasa, a pilot using the airport in the summer wrote, “Please do something,” after air traffic controllers failed to provide appropriate guidance about multiple nearby aircraft. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ LaGuardia pilots raised safety alarms months before deadly runway crash

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 23 March: Civilians bear brunt of ongoing war
events.description: The war in the Middle East is well into its fourth week and the humanitarian emergency it has sparked continues to be the focus of international attention, along with the energy crisis caused by attacks on shipping in key Strait of Hormuz and other oil and gas infrastructure in the Gulf region. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 23 March: Civilians bear brunt of ongoing war

events.title: Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on
events.description: President’s declaration allows officials to tackle fuel hoarding or profiteering, while energy secretary says country will lean more heavily on coal Middle East crisis – live updates The Philippines president, Ferdinand Marcos, has declared a state of “national energy emergency” as a result of the Middle East war , which his administration said posed “an imminent danger of a critically low energy supply”. The state of emergency, which will initially last for a year, was declared just hours after the country’s energy secretary said the Philippines planned to boost the output of its coal-fired power plants to keep electricity costs down as the war wreaks havoc with gas shipments. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on

events.title: War in the Middle East: Iran nuclear facility hit as equivalent of ‘one classroom of children’ killed, wounded daily in Lebanon
events.description: More than 1,000 people have been killed and 2,584 injured in Lebanon since the start of the US-Israel war on Iran, UN officials said Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in the Middle East: Iran nuclear facility hit as equivalent of ‘one classroom of children’ killed, wounded daily in Lebanon

events.title: Strike on Sudan hospital kills at least 64 and wounds 89 more, WHO reports
events.description: Victims of army drone attack on East Darfur health facility included children and medical personnel A strike on a healthcare facility in Sudan has killed 64 people and wounded 89 more, the World Health Organization reported on Saturday. The UN’s humanitarian office in Sudan had earlier said it was “appalled by the attack on a hospital in East Darfur yesterday, reportedly killing dozens, including children, and injuring more”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strike on Sudan hospital kills at least 64 and wounds 89 more, WHO reports

events.title: Australia prepares for oil prices above US$120 a barrel as diesel passes $3 a litre nationwide
events.description: Fuel stocks at same level as when US and Israel launched their war on Iran a month ago, energy minister says Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The federal government is preparing for scenarios where global oil prices spike above US$120 a barrel, as diesel prices nationwide go past $3 a litre and the prime minister, Anthony Albanese, plans another national cabinet meeting to plan petrol supply. The treasurer, Jim Chalmers, said one option under consideration was expanding ethanol mandates across the country, after Guardian Australia revealed the government was analysing increasing the biofuel’s supply . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia prepares for oil prices above US$120 a barrel as diesel passes $3 a litre nationwide

events.title: Japan to begin biggest-ever oil release from national reserves as Middle East energy crisis bites
events.description: PM Sanae Takaichi says about 80m barrels of stockpiled oil to be provided to refiners – equivalent to 45 days of domestic demand • Middle East crisis – live updates Japan will begin the biggest-ever release of oil from its strategic reserves this week, the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi , has said, as the country braces for possible shortages caused by the US-Israel war on Iran . The government last week approved the release of 15 days’ worth of private-sector reserves, amid concern that the conflict in the Middle East will continue to hinder the flow of tanker traffic along the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan to begin biggest-ever oil release from national reserves as Middle East energy crisis bites

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge
events.description: The Security Council is met for an open briefing on Ukraine, requested by European members following Kyiv’s 18 March letter citing a surge in Russian strikes. Inside the country, attacks continue to take a heavy toll: since 19 March, at least 25 civilians have reportedly been killed and more than 130 injured, including children, particularly in Donetsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia. We’ll have live coverage below, app users can follow here – and for all our key meetings coverage each day, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 March: West Bank attacks and Lebanon in focus as Security Council meets
events.description: As the war continues to roil the Middle East and compound suffering for civilians across the region, the economic ramifications of the emergency are still playing out, with the Strait of Hormuz the focus of global attention with crude oil prices surging over $100 a barrel again. Meanwhile, settler attacks have escalated dramatically against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, a topic that we'll be across today also, with aid updates and live reporting from the Security Council. UN News app users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 March: West Bank attacks and Lebanon in focus as Security Council meets

events.title: Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks
events.description: Pakistan, the only nuclear-armed Muslim state, faces mounting pressure as its Saudi defense pact and Iran ties clash amid the intensifying regional war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks

events.title: Ghana signs security partnership with EU
events.description: In tonight's edition: Ghana has become the first African country to enter into a landmark security and defence partnership with the European Union. Also, with several African countries heavily reliant on fertiliser imported from the Gulf, there's been a knock-on effect on the continent as the war in Iran chokes traditional supply routes. Plus the death toll from heavy flooding in Kenya reaches 88, with at least 21 counties affected.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Ghana signs security partnership with EU

events.title: WHO verifies deadly hospital attack in war-torn Sudan
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) verified on Saturday a hospital attack in war-torn Sudan that killed 64 people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO verifies deadly hospital attack in war-torn Sudan

events.title: Analysts say Gaza 'civilian' deaths include Hamas, other terror members working as medics, media workers
events.description: Researchers say Hamas and PIJ martyr lists reveal Gaza terrorists who posed as doctors, nurses and journalists, clouding civilian casualty figures.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Analysts say Gaza 'civilian' deaths include Hamas, other terror members working as medics, media workers

events.title: Middle East live: Israeli strikes on South Lebanon leave nine dead
events.description: In Lebanon, Israeli strikes killed at least six people in a town and a Palestinian refugee camp in the southern Sidon area, and three more in another town. This comes as US President Donald Trump expresses optimism at ending the war, after sending a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East live: Israeli strikes on South Lebanon leave nine dead

events.title: 'The humanitarian crisis here is worsening by the minute': Israel continues to bomb Lebanon
events.description: More than a million people have been driven from their homes in Lebanon as Israel continues its bombardment of the country and seizes land south of the Litani River. FRANCE 24 correspondent Renée Davis reports from Beirut on the rising resentment towards Israel's ongoing air strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'The humanitarian crisis here is worsening by the minute': Israel continues to bomb Lebanon

events.title: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over 24-hour period
events.description: Cities across Ukraine were hit, with local officials reporting at least eight people killed and dozens injured.

events.title: Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis
events.description: Since the start of the Middle East conflict with Israeli and US strikes on Iran on 28 February, concerns have been growing over rising oil and commodity prices.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis

events.title: Boss of financial giant BlackRock warns of global recession if oil price hits $150
events.description: Larry Fink says if oil prices stay high for a sustained period it will have "profound implications" for the world economy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Boss of financial giant BlackRock warns of global recession if oil price hits $150

events.title: At least 66 dead after military plane crashes in Colombia
events.description: At least 66 people have been killed in a military plane crash in Colombia, the country's armed forces chief has said.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ At least 66 dead after military plane crashes in Colombia

events.title: Iran threatens mass ‘water war’ with strikes on key plants in days, UN official warns
events.description: Iran threatens to target desalination and energy infrastructure within days, a U.N. official warns, citing lasting consequences for global markets and water supplies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens mass ‘water war’ with strikes on key plants in days, UN official warns

events.title: ‘A miracle’: Canadian flight attendant ejected from plane survives New York crash
events.description: Solange Tremblay was ejected over 100 metres from the plane after collision at LaGuardia airport, her daughter says Two pilots killed after Air Canada jet collision at LaGuardia in New York Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A flight attendant on the Air Canada Jazz flight that collided with a fire truck at New York ’s LaGuardia airport on Sunday survived in what her daughter called a “complete miracle”, when she was ejected more than 100 metres from the plane while still strapped to her seat. The CRJ-900 jet, operated by Jazz Aviation, collided with a fire truck as it landed, killing both the pilot and co-pilot. Nine people were sent to the hospital with injuries, including Solange Tremblay, a flight attendant. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ ‘A miracle’: Canadian flight attendant ejected from plane survives New York crash

events.title: Albanese urged to help Australians struggling with fuel crisis, as NZ offers first-of-its-kind cash relief
events.description: David Pocock says a flat 25% export levy on gas producers could redirect ‘wartime profits’ to struggling Australians Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pressure is mounting on the Albanese government to help households struggling with fuel prices, with working from home and free public transport posited as possible solutions. Nearly 150,000 New Zealand families will soon receive a weekly cash payment to help them afford petrol , believed to be the world’s first fuel relief package that directly pays citizens since the Israel-US war on Iran began. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Albanese urged to help Australians struggling with fuel crisis, as NZ offers first-of-its-kind cash relief

events.title: Denmark's Frederiksen weakened by election setback, coalition talks loom
events.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen faced a sharp electoral setback Tuesday as her Social Democrats slid toward their worst result in more than a century. Despite broad international backing for her firm stance toward Washington over Greenland, domestic concerns over migration and the cost of living dominated the vote, leaving Frederiksen heading into uncertain coalition talks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Denmark's Frederiksen weakened by election setback, coalition talks loom

events.title: UN calls for solidarity and political will to stamp out racial discrimination
events.description: Nearly 70 years after South African police fired upon peaceful demonstrators in Sharpeville protesting apartheid-era laws, killing 69, the UN renewed the commitment to work for justice and equality on Monday, marking the International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.

events.title: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz is an international crisis
events.description: As such, it requires urgent action.

events.title: New Zealand footballers willing to play Iran World Cup match outside US
events.description: The teams are set to play on June 15 in Los Angeles, but Iran has threatened to boycott its matches due to the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Zealand footballers willing to play Iran World Cup match outside US

events.title: Moldova imposes 60-day energy emergency after Russian strikes in Ukraine cut key power line
events.description: Moldova’s Parliament voted on Tuesday in favor of imposing a 60-day state of emergency in the country’s energy sector after Russian strikes on neighboring Ukraine’s energy grid disconnected a key power line linking Moldova to Romania
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Moldova imposes 60-day energy emergency after Russian strikes in Ukraine cut key power line

events.title: UK nuclear submarine deployed to Arabian Sea before Iran targets key US-UK base: reports
events.description: Iranian FM Seyed Abbas Araghchi warned Britain against supporting U.S. military action, calling its decision to allow U.S. forces to use its bases "participation in aggression."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK nuclear submarine deployed to Arabian Sea before Iran targets key US-UK base: reports

events.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
events.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies

events.title: Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians
events.description: The death toll from a horrific attack on a hospital in Sudan’s Darfur has risen further, amid a “sharp increase” in drone attacks against civilians this year, UN agencies said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians

events.title: War in the Middle East: Cultural treasures damaged, with more at risk
events.description: Since the outbreak of war on 28 February, several unique sites of cultural significance have been damaged in Iran, Israel and Lebanon, alongside immense suffering, displacement and death.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in the Middle East: Cultural treasures damaged, with more at risk

events.title: Trump, Starmer agree Strait of Hormuz must reopen as Middle East conflict escalates
events.description: Strait of Hormuz closure dominates a Trump-Starmer call, as Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran warns of further U.S. strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump, Starmer agree Strait of Hormuz must reopen as Middle East conflict escalates

events.title: Iran chokes Strait of Hormuz with reported $2M tanker toll, regime threatens global oil supply
events.description: Strait of Hormuz traffic is near collapse as Iran reportedly charges tankers $2 million in transit fees, prompting President Trump to threaten strikes on Iranian power infrastructure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran chokes Strait of Hormuz with reported $2M tanker toll, regime threatens global oil supply

events.title: Germany news: Race against time for stranded humpback whale
events.description: Efforts to free a humpback whale trapped in shallow waters have so far failed, but wheels are in motion to free the creature. Meanwhile, police are guarding a nuclear waste convoy in western Germany. DW has the latest.

events.title: Iran war: US to deploy thousands more soldiers — reports
events.description: The US combat forces are expected to come from the 82nd Airborne Division, which is capable of deploying anywhere in the world in under 24 hours. Meanwhile, Iran said it will fight "until complete victory."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: US to deploy thousands more soldiers — reports

events.title: 'Every indicator is flashing red,' says UN as it warns of record 'climate imbalance'
events.description: The Earth's climate is in a "state of emergency", according to the United Nations which has warned it is more out of balance than at any other time in observed history.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Every indicator is flashing red,' says UN as it warns of record 'climate imbalance'

events.title: 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes
events.description: Joseph M. Humire, acting assistant secretary of War for Homeland Defense and Americas Security Affairs, explains the Trump administration's strategy for the Western Hemisphere.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes

events.title: UK counterterrorism police probe antisemitic arson attack as Iran-linked group claims responsibility
events.description: An Iran-linked group claims responsibility for torching Jewish ambulances outside a Golders Green synagogue in London as counterterrorism police investigate.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK counterterrorism police probe antisemitic arson attack as Iran-linked group claims responsibility

events.title: Eight arrested for ‘brutal’ attack on capybara in Brazil
events.description: In incident filmed by security cameras in Rio de Janeiro, group of attackers beat animal with sticks and iron bars Police in Rio de Janeiro have arrested eight people for brutally beating a capybara – the world’s largest rodent. Resembling a giant guinea pig, the light brown capybara ( Hydrochoerus hydrochaeris ) is often seen roaming the Brazilian city, particularly near streams and lagoons. Continue reading...

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle intensifies off WA as it continues rare path across Australia
events.description: Storm is not likely to make a direct hit on Perth, though the city may experience heavy rainfall on Friday and Saturday Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tropical Cyclone Narelle was again intensifying into a severe storm off Western Australia’s Kimberley coast on Wednesday with communities in the state’s world heritage-listed Shark Bay preparing for a potential direct hit on Friday night. Narelle had made a rare trip for a cyclone system by forming in the Coral Sea off Queensland and maintaining structure all the way west to the Indian Ocean, where it was expected to build into a major category four system. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Tropical Cyclone Narelle intensifies off WA as it continues rare path across Australia

events.title: War in the Middle East: An opportunity for Iran's minorities?
events.description: Shiite Persians make up 51 percent of Iran's population. Yet communities like the Azeris, Kurds, Balochs and Arabs also number in the millions. Historically, they have often been ignored or mistreated by the regime in Tehran, both under the shah and the Islamic Republic. These ethnic minorities could play a role in the current conflict raging in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's David Gilbert and Florent Marchais report.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in the Middle East: An opportunity for Iran's minorities?

events.title: A city opens its doors to the world: New York and the United Nations
events.description: Born from the ashes of the Second World War, the dream of a more peaceful and fairer world drew nations together in 1945 under a new vision for humanity: the United Nations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A city opens its doors to the world: New York and the United Nations

events.title: Pilots killed in LaGuardia plane crash named
events.description: Investigators released details from the final three minutes of cockpit and tower communications, which showed controllers cleared both the plane and a fire truck to cross the runway.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Pilots killed in LaGuardia plane crash named

events.title: From Pakistan to Egypt, Iran war drives up prices in Global South
events.description: Developing economies in Asia, Africa and the Middle East are most exposed to surging energy costs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From Pakistan to Egypt, Iran war drives up prices in Global South

events.title: BBC and NWA: the day ABC staff went on strike – and left Aunty looking ‘a bit different’
events.description: Triple J signed off with the hip-hop anthem Express Yourself while other radio and TV networks filled the air with BBC broadcasts, re-runs and soothing music Sign up for Guardian Australia’s free weekly media newsletter here Broadcasters had warned their audiences that the ABC would look “a bit different” on Wednesday – and as the clock struck 11am, they weren’t wrong. As more than 2,000 ABC staff walked off the job for the first time in two decades in protest of their working conditions, the public broadcaster’s news channel switched over to the BBC . Sign up to get Guardian Australia’s weekly media diary as a free newsletter Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 BBC and NWA: the day ABC staff went on strike – and left Aunty looking ‘a bit different’

events.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
events.description: In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status

events.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
events.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies

events.title: Estonia and Latvia say they were hit by drones as Ukraine unleashes massive attack on Russia
events.description: A drone coming from Russian airspace struck a power plant in Estonia on Wednesday, while another crashed into Latvian territory, with officials in Riga saying it was likely a Ukrainian drone gone astray. Kyiv earlier launched a massive drone barrage against Russia, sparking a fire at a major petroleum export hub not far from the Estonian border.

events.title: US proposes 15-point plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' oil vessels
events.description: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday he had sent a peace plan to Iran, expressing cautious optimism about ending nearly a month of war. In a potential sign of de-escalation, Tehran announced it would allow “non-hostile” vessels to pass through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a key artery for global oil supplies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US proposes 15-point plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' oil vessels

events.title: UN weather agency warns of record ‘climate imbalance’ as planetary warming accelerates
events.description: All-time high greenhouse gas concentrations in Earth’s atmosphere continue to drive heat records on land and sea, with long-lasting consequences for humanity, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) warned on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN weather agency warns of record ‘climate imbalance’ as planetary warming accelerates

events.title: NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive
events.description: Russia launched nearly 400 drones and missiles at Ukraine, prompting Poland and Romania to scramble NATO fighter jets as strikes neared their airspace.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive

events.title: US Democrats condemn war on Iran as Senate resolution fails
events.description: The latest war powers resolution in the US Senate to end US attacks on Iran has failed in a 47-53 vote.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US Democrats condemn war on Iran as Senate resolution fails

events.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks?
events.description: Iran, Israel, the Gulf faced new missiles and drones. The US has signalled military escalation and openness to a deal.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks?

events.title: Rescuers fail to free stranded whale stuck on German coast
events.description: A whale is fighting for survival off Germany's Baltic coast after becoming trapped in shallow waters. Initial efforts to rescue the stranded animal failed, but new equipment is on the way for another try.

events.title: Iran war puts South Asia's Gulf remittances at risk
events.description: Millions of South Asian workers in the Middle East provide a vital economic boost by sending money home. Economists warn these flows would be at risk if a protracted conflict dents the Gulf region's economies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war puts South Asia's Gulf remittances at risk

events.title: Lebanon's humanitarian crisis worsens as fighting continues
events.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah is continuing and Israel has announced it may occupy Lebanon's south long term. What is at stake for hundreds of thousands displaced Lebanese civilians?

events.title: Iran war pushes plastic packaging costs higher
events.description: Oil prices rose again on Tuesday, a day after a sharp decline, amid confusion over which way the Iran war is going. Brent crude settled well above $100 a barrel. Meanwhile, rising energy costs stemming from the conflict are causing the cost of plastic packaging to skyrocket, which in turn will make everything on supermarket shelves more expensive. But first: the EU and Australia have sealed a landmark free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on most goods.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war pushes plastic packaging costs higher

events.title: Strikes hit Iran and Tehran targets Israel and Gulf states amid mixed signals over talks to end war
events.description: Airstrikes battered Iran and Iranian missiles and drones targeted Israel’s Tel Aviv and sites across the Mideast
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strikes hit Iran and Tehran targets Israel and Gulf states amid mixed signals over talks to end war

events.title: Preliminary magnitude 7.6 earthquake strikes near Tonga in South Pacific Ocean: USGS
events.description: The USGS says a strong preliminary magnitude 7.6 earthquake struck near Tonga in the South Pacific Ocean on Tuesday
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Preliminary magnitude 7.6 earthquake strikes near Tonga in South Pacific Ocean: USGS

events.title: Russia fires nearly 400 drones at Ukraine with signs its spring offensive has started
events.description: Russia has unleashed a massive drone and missile barrage on civilian areas of Ukraine and is stepping up ground attacks along the front
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia fires nearly 400 drones at Ukraine with signs its spring offensive has started

events.title: How Kyrgyzstan built a system to support domestic violence survivors
events.description: Domestic violence was not something people spoke about openly in Kyrgyzstan in the 1990s following the collapse of the Soviet Union, but after a long road of dedicated efforts, there are now laws addressing family abuse, crisis centres and hotlines while human rights defenders tackle such new challenges as sexual slavery.

events.title: FBI rescues missing autistic teen after two days lost in frigid wilderness
events.description: FBI agents rescued missing autistic teen Joel Medina in New Jersey after a nearly two-day search in near-freezing temperatures and rugged woods.

events.title: Body of 20-year-old missing student canoer recovered after multiday search on Connecticut lake
events.description: Dominick Tocci, a 20-year-old College of the Holy Cross junior, was found dead in Lake Hayward after his canoe capsized in East Haddam, Connecticut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Body of 20-year-old missing student canoer recovered after multiday search on Connecticut lake

events.title: Authorities share update on circumstances around missing college student Jimmy Gracey's death
events.description: University of Alabama student Jimmy Gracey, 20, went missing in Barcelona during Spring Break. His body was found near a nightclub.

events.title: Churchill, Shakespeare and the UK flag all under siege in modern Britain, commentators say
events.description: Britain's statues, flags and icons like Churchill and Shakespeare are under attack, as commentators warn DEI ideology is erasing the nation's history.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Churchill, Shakespeare and the UK flag all under siege in modern Britain, commentators say

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Queensland government scraps emissions target for Brisbane Olympics – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Australia prepares for oil prices above US$120 a barrel as diesel passes $3 a litre nationwide Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The government has announced the federal and Queensland governments will each chip in $1bn to Rio Tinto’s Boyne aluminium smelter in central Queensland, to keep it viable into the future. The government says the move, which will include Rio “underwriting significant investment in energy and transmission”, will unlock almost $7.5bn in investment in Queensland. With a considerable public investment, we are catalysing a fourfold private investment that will build out the renewable energy grid and keep thousands of good regional jobs in central Queensland. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran war: Tehran says US is 'negotiating with itself'
events.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry has said it is not negotiating with the US. It comes after Trump reportedly put forward a proposal including a one-month ceasefire, conveyed to Iran via Pakistan. Follow DW for more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Tehran says US is 'negotiating with itself'

events.title: Danish PM's left bloc leads election, but no majority
events.description: Denmark's left bloc headed by Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen took an early lead in Tuesday's general election but failed to secure a majority, exit polls showed, with Frederiksen's Social Democrats posting their weakest showing in 125 years. FRANCE 24's Haxie Meyers-Belkin speaks with Ulrik Pram Gad, Senior Researcher at the Danish Institute for International Studies.

events.title: Russia says it shot down almost 400 Ukrainian drones as Moscow, Kyiv escalate strikes
events.description: Russia is reporting that it has shot down hundreds of Ukrainian drones in a massive overnight attack across its regions and Crimea
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia says it shot down almost 400 Ukrainian drones as Moscow, Kyiv escalate strikes

events.title: Gaza: Commitment to US-backed plan crucial to recovery, Security Council hears
events.description: As tensions escalate in the Middle East, the international community must not lose sight of the situation in Gaza, an official with US President Donald Trump’s Board of Peace across the shattered enclave said on Tuesday in his first appearance in the UN Security Council.

events.title: Marcos promises 'flow of oil' as Philippines declares energy emergency
events.description: The President says the country will procure one million barrels of oil to add to the current stock.

events.title: ‘Extraordinary event’ for mountain gorillas as new twins born in DRC
events.description: Conservationists celebrate second twin birth just two months after another set discovered in Virunga national park A second set of mountain gorilla twins has been born in Virunga national park in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), in what conservationists are celebrating as an “extraordinary” event for the endangered primates. Just two months after tiny twin mountain gorillas were discovered by rangers in the Virunga massif, in eastern DRC, another rare twin birth has been found by park wardens. This time, an infant male and female have been spotted in the Baraka family, a troop of 19 mountain gorillas that roam the region’s high-altitude rainforests. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Extraordinary event’ for mountain gorillas as new twins born in DRC

events.title: ‘Massive betrayal’: Iranian nationals with valid tourist visas blocked from entering Australia for six months
events.description: Tony Burke says decisions about permanent stays should be ‘deliberate decisions of the government, not a random consequence of who booked a holiday’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Iranian tourists will be banned from entering Australia for the next six months after the home affairs minister, Tony Burke, triggered tough new immigration laws over concerns visitors may not be able to return to Iran. The ban could apply to up to 7,200 Iranians with valid tourist visas – though some may still be given the chance to enter the country under special consideration. Continue reading...

events.title: Vietnam and Russia advance nuclear power deal as energy security concerns grow
events.description: Vietnam and Russia have signed a deal to build a nuclear power plant, marking a revival of Hanoi’s nuclear ambitions

events.title: US talking to itself, says Iran as Trump claims wheels of diplomacy turning

events.title: Some of the world’s poorest countries to lose UK aid due to 56% budget cut
events.description: UK’s bilateral aid to African countries, which funds areas such as schools and clinics, to be cut by almost £900m by 2028-29 Some of the world’s poorest countries will lose out on UK aid that funds programmes such as schools and clinics, due to budget cuts set out by the foreign secretary. The UK’s bilateral aid to African countries will be reduced by almost £900m by 2028-29 – a 56% cut – as part of more than £6bn in cuts which are funding an increase in defence spending. Continue reading...

events.title: Canadian woman held with daughter by ICE warns all immigrants to ‘lie low’
events.description: Tania Warner says she has documents showing she is in the US legally, but immigration agents were not swayed Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A Canadian woman who has been imprisoned with her seven-year-old daughter by Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) has cautioned other immigrants that they are at risk of detention, even if they follow the correct legal process – and warned them to keep out of sight for as long as Donald Trump is president. “Don’t go anywhere near a checkpoint, and if your papers are in processing, just lay low. Trump meant what he said – he is trying to get rid of everyone, whether they are good or bad,” said Tania Warner, 47, who is currently held with her autistic daughter, Ayla, at the Dilley immigration processing center in south Texas. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canadian woman held with daughter by ICE warns all immigrants to ‘lie low’

events.title: In Iran, hope gives way to fear as conflict rages on
events.description: Nearly a month after the US-Israeli strikes on Iran began, many who hoped the regime would soon fall have been left disappointed. DW spoke to Iranians about how the regime's survival has dashed those early hopes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 In Iran, hope gives way to fear as conflict rages on

events.title: Body discovered after second fire in arson attack house

events.title: Driver hops curb, strikes 9 students during after school pickup in Iowa

events.title: US sends 15-point peace plan to Iran while deploying troops in the region
events.description: The Trump administration is ramping up efforts to end the Middle East war. After Trump said talks were ongoing between Washington and Tehran, Pakistani officials said that the White House had sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran - yet Tehran has dismissed the idea of negotiating with the US. Meanwhile, US media reported that at least 1,000 troops are to deploy to the Middle East. Although it could be to put pressure on Tehran, Iran is also preparing for the possibility of US troops on the ground. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani looks at what we know from those negotiations, and what they mean for both parties.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US sends 15-point peace plan to Iran while deploying troops in the region

events.title: Colombia issues arrest warrants for rebel group members for Miguel Uribe killing

events.title: Forests are more than a resource: How Belarus protects its vital ecosystem

events.title: Girl, Interrupted: Afghan girls and women shut out of education, careers, and public life
events.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Mursal Sayas, radio producer/podcaster for Shakar, and a women’s rights activist. Once a pathway to individual & collective development, education has been abruptly stripped away ffom millions of Afghan girls. Ms. Sayas warns that this exclusion extends far beyond schooling, reaching into what she describes as the near erasure of women from public life. The mechanisms of control, she explains, are visible and intimate. They shape Afghan institutions and the dynamics within families and communities, in turn destabilising the very fabric of society.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Girl, Interrupted: Afghan girls and women shut out of education, careers, and public life

events.title: Jailed Bolsonaro granted ‘humanitarian house arrest’ amid failing health
events.description: Former Brazil president, serving 27 years over attempted coup, given initial 90-day period that could be extended Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Brazil ’s former president Jair Bolsonaro has been granted permission to serve his 27-year sentence for a coup attempt at home instead of in prison because of his failing health. The decision by supreme court justice Alexandre de Moraes followed Bolsonaro’s hospitalization since 13 March for pneumonia, one of several health problems the former leader has faced since he was stabbed by a man in 2018 before he was elected president. Continue reading...

events.title: Stocks rise and oil dips on hopes of 15-point Iran peace plan
events.description: Markets in Asia and Europe move higher after Iran says it will permit ‘non-hostile’ ships through strait of Hormuz Business live – latest updates The price of oil has dipped and Asian and European stock markets have moved higher after reports that Donald Trump has sent a 15-point framework for peace to Iran, amid hopes of a ceasefire in the Middle East. Positive sentiment may also have been boosted by reports that Iran had announced it was permitting “non-hostile” ships to pass safely through the strait of Hormuz, in a move that could help to reopen the vital shipping lane. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Stocks rise and oil dips on hopes of 15-point Iran peace plan

events.title: French municipal vote: What do results mean for 2027 ballot?

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: US ramps up military presence in the Middle East amid reported talks
events.description: The United States is ramping up its military presence in the Middle East even more, despite the US having reportedly sent a 15-point peace plan to Tehran. There are plans to send soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division to support operations against Iran, according to US media reports. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the region by March 25.

events.title: Football: France arrive in Boston to face Brazil and Colombia in friendlies
events.description: The French national team have arrived in Boston for their US tour. France will face Brazil on March 26 and Colombia on March 29 in friendly matches.

events.title: US to deploy elite army brigade to Middle East: reports
events.description: The United States is planning to send some 3,000 soldiers from the elite 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East to support operations against Iran, US media reported Tuesday. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the Middle East by Friday, the latest deadline set by President Donald Trump for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Wall Street Journal. The Guardian Washington bureau chief David Smith reports.

events.title: Danish PM faces fight to stay in post after election losses
events.description: Denmark's centre-left prime minister has said she wants to stay in her job after inconclusive election results left the country facing weeks of coalition talks.

events.title: Denmark goes to the polls in a snap election after Trump's Greenland bid

events.title: If Cuba falls, who steps in? Castro dynasty shadows island’s future

events.title: Iran’s new security boss Mohammad Zolghadr: Why his appointment matters
events.description: Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC commander, will manage Iran's security amid external US-Israeli pressures and internal unrest.

events.title: Madagascar’s military ruler decrees that ministers must pass lie detector tests
events.description: Michael Randrianirina, who sacked PM and cabinet without explanation, claims measure is to root out corruption Madagascar’s military president has said new ministers will have to pass lie detector tests to root out corrupt candidates, after he dismissed the prime minister and cabinet without explanation earlier this month. Michael Randrianirina came to power in a coup in October after weeks of youth-led protests under the banner “Gen Z Madagascar”. However, young people were quickly disenchanted by his choice of government officials, which they saw as being part of the old, corrupt elite. Continue reading...

events.title: Will President Trump act on his threat to take Cuba?
events.description: New Yorker writer Jon Lee Anderson describes conditions in Cuba, why it's vulnerable now — and what regime change would mean — considering the Castro family's entrenchment in the Cuban government.

events.title: Japan says Chinese Embassy break-in 'regrettable'

events.title: Who is Trump talking to in Iran?

events.title: The UN housing development which challenged 1940s’ segregated United States

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand

events.title: More young people want to vote in New Zealand’s Māori electorates. What are they and how do they work?
events.description: Growing numbers of young voters are signing up to the Māori electoral roll as debate flares over the need for dedicated seats ahead of November’s election More young people have signed up to vote in Māori electorates, new figures from the electoral commission show, as New Zealand prepares for an election this year. The rise comes after years of tense relations between Indigenous New Zealanders and the centre-right coalition government. The latest figures show 58% of eligible 18- to 24-year-olds have registered for the Māori roll, up from 50% in 2023. Continue reading...

events.title: Judge grants $1 murder bond for Georgia woman accused of using pills to induce second-trimester abortion

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
events.description: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news

events.title: Lebo M sues comedian Learnmore Jonasi claiming Circle of Life misrepresentation
events.description: Grammy winner seeks more than $20m in damages over mistranslation of The Lion King chant A Grammy-winning South African composer who wrote and performed the opening chant in Circle of Life for Disney’s The Lion King is suing a comedian for allegedly damaging his reputation by intentionally misrepresenting the song’s meaning on a podcast and in his standup routine. Lebohang Morake’s lawsuit accuses the Zimbabwean comedian Learnmore Mwanyenyeka, known as Learnmore Jonasi, of intentionally mistranslating the chant, which launches the 1994 movie and is central to staged versions as well as Disney’s 2019 remake. Continue reading...

events.title: US and China both believe the other is a declining power
events.description: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.

events.title: WNBA's labor deal is a structural shift for women's sports
events.description: America's top women's basketball league just agreed to a new CBA. It will have a significant impact on women's sport.

events.title: Meta hit with $375 million fine in child exploitation case
events.description: While the New Mexico jury's landmark decision to hold Meta liable could be a bellwether, the penalty is only a fraction of what prosecutors sought.

events.title: Danish PM's left-wing bloc wins vote but with no majority
events.description: It is unclear if Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen can secure a third term, as her party came in first but recorded a poor result. Foreign Minister Rasmussen became a kingmaker ahead of thorny negotiations.

events.title: NASA plans moon base instead of orbital lunar station
events.description: New NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman says the US space agency will pivot from launching an orbiting lunar station to constructing a base on the moon's surface. It comes amid a renewed moon race with Russia and China.

events.title: Who wins what in the EU's new trade deal with Australia?
events.description: Squeezed by Trump's tariffs and China's industrial muscle, the EU and Australia bury old trade disputes to finalize a long-delayed free trade deal.

events.title: Denmark's Frederiksen weakened by election setback, coalition talks loom
events.description: Denmark's Social Democrats, led by Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen, finished first in Tuesday's general election but posted their weakest showing in more than 120 years and the left-wing bloc failed to secure a majority.

events.title: Hungary is 'the Kremlin's biggest agent in the EU', analyst says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Monte Francis, Dr Mark Loustau, Fellow at the Institute for Advanced Study at Central European University, asserts that "we can probably safely say that Hungary is the Kremllin's biggest agent in the EU" adding that "for years now Orban has done the bidding" of Moscow "in trying to destabilise not only unity in the EU but also the government in Ukraine". Hungarian foreign minister Peter Szijjarto is alleged to have leaked sensitive information about EU negotiations to Russia.

events.title: Has the AFCON trophy been hidden at a military base in Senegal? No
events.description: In the wake of the Confederation of African Football's recent decision to strip Senegal of its AFCON title and declare Morocco the winners, some online users are claiming that the AFCON trophy has been hidden away in a military base in Senegal. But as FRANCE 24's Charlotte Hughes explains, this rumour is not true, and the footage some internet users have been using to make the false claim has been taken out of its original context.

events.title: Meta ordered to pay $375 million over child exploitation in landmark verdict
events.description: A New Mexico jury on Tuesday found social media giant Meta liable for making children vulnerable to predators and other online dangers across its platforms, marking one of the first major jury rulings on child safety in the social media era.

events.title: Wildlife Photographer of the Year: Pick your favourite snap
events.description: The 2026 Wildlife Photographer of the Year has named its People's Choice winner, selected from more than 60,000 entries and voted for by the public.

events.title: Italian politician resigns over stake in mafia-linked restaurant
events.description: Italian justice undersecretary Andrea Delmastro has resigned after it emerged he had held a stake in a restaurant linked to the mafia.

events.title: Owner of OnlyFans adult platform dies at the age of 43
events.description: Leonid Radvinsky, a Ukrainian-American entrepreneur who was the owner of the adult content platform OnlyFans, has died.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Owner of OnlyFans adult platform dies at the age of 43

events.title: South Africa arrests 12 senior police officers on suspicion of corruption
events.description: South African prosecutors say anti-graft investigators have arrested 12 senior police officers over alleged corruption and fraud tied to a police services contract
events.ticker_label: 💥 South Africa arrests 12 senior police officers on suspicion of corruption

events.title: EU and Australia sign free trade pact and announce a new defense partnership
events.description: The European Union and Australia have agreed on the final text of a free trade agreement some two years after negotiations broke down over Australian demands for more red meat market access and complaints about Australian products labeled with traditio...

events.title: As US pressure grows for leadership change in Cuba, a Castro could be the next president
events.description: Speculation is mounting about who, if anyone, might replace Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel as U.S. President Donald Trump pushes for change in Cuba’s leadership

events.title: European Union and Australia agree on text of free trade pact and announce a new defense partnership
events.description: European Union and Australia agree on text of free trade pact and announce a new defense partnership

events.title: A pink veil across the fields: Thousands flock to Greece’s peach blossoms
events.description: A veil of blossoming trees unfolds each spring across the plains of Greece’s largest peach-producing region

events.title: A 1,000-year-old cliff-top temple lies battered after Cambodia-Thailand border clashes
events.description: Cambodia says fighting with Thailand has left the Preah Vihear temple badly damaged and too dangerous for visitors

events.title: Cancer nurse turned archbishop celebrates election as first woman to lead Church of England
events.description: Sarah Mullally, a one-time cancer nurse who became a priest at the age of 40, will be installed as archbishop of Canterbury

events.title: Haitian gangs expand reach as police are accused of ‘summary executions’
events.description: Deadly gangs in Haiti are expanding their reach to include control over key sea and road routes as police in the beleaguered Caribbean island nation are being accused of using “unnecessary and disproportionate lethal force and summary executions.”

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: Cameras capture truckers unable to read road signs, answer basic questions during Florida crackdown
events.description: Troopers say up to half of truckers at some Florida weigh stations fail English proficiency tests as the Trump administration ramps up Department of Transportation enforcement.

events.title: Ex-'American Idol' contestant faces new charges over alleged murder of wife in Ohio home
events.description: Former "American Idol" contestant Caleb Flynn has been indicted on aggravated murder and 10 additional charges in the alleged killing of his Ohio wife.

events.title: DHS touts ICE nabbing illegal migrant leaving police station accused of raping 5-year-old girl
events.description: ICE arrested Carlos Aguilar Reynoso, accused of raping a Long Island girl, after authorities used a desk-appearance ticket to bypass NY sanctuary laws.

events.title: 'House of horrors' suspect accused of holding stepson captive hit with new charges as she denies allegations
events.description: Kimberly Sullivan, accused of holding her stepson captive for decades in a Connecticut home, now faces additional kidnapping and assault charges.

events.title: EU blocks US vote to define gender as biological men and women at UN women’s forum
events.description: The EU used a procedural motion to block a U.S. resolution at the United Nations that sought to define gender as referring to biological men and women, leaving it without a vote.

events.title: Carlos Fernández de Cossío: Cuba 'preparing' for 'possibility of military aggression'
events.description: Cuba's deputy foreign minister says the military is preparing for possible U.S. aggression after Trump said it would be an "honor" to take Cuba.
